question: How much does Consuelo cost?
reply: The power dialer is $20/seat/mo on the Growth plan. The AI CRM is free forever — no trial expiration, no feature gates. Most teams replace $250+/seat stacks (Salesforce + PhoneBurner + Gong) with one $20/seat platform.

question: Is there a free trial?
reply: Yes — 14-day free trial of the Growth plan, no credit card required. You get full access to the power dialer, AI coaching, and analytics. After the trial, you can stay on the free Starter plan or upgrade.

question: What's included in the free Starter plan?
reply: 1 seat, power dialer, full CRM, 50 AI coaching minutes per month, and basic analytics. Enough to run a solo operation. Upgrade to Growth when you need multi-line dialing, team management, and unlimited coaching.

question: Are there contracts or cancellation fees?
reply: No contracts, cancel anytime. Monthly billing, no annual commitment required. We keep customers by being useful, not by locking them in.

question: Do you charge per minute for calls?
reply: Calling minutes are billed separately through Twilio at cost — no markup. You pay Twilio directly for usage. Consuelo's $20/seat covers the dialer, CRM, coaching, and analytics.

question: How does Consuelo compare to PhoneBurner or Kixie?
reply: PhoneBurner is $149/seat, Kixie is $95/seat — neither includes a CRM or AI coaching. Consuelo is $20/seat with a free CRM, real-time coaching, and analytics included. You get more for less.

question: What is local presence dialing?
reply: Calls display a local number matching your prospect's area code. Higher answer rates because people pick up local numbers. Consuelo matches by geo-proximity — exact area code first, then nearest within 100 miles.

question: How does real-time AI coaching work?
reply: On-call whispers give reps live guidance while they're on the phone — objection handling, script prompts, compliance reminders. Upload your own scripts for training. Post-call scoring shows what worked and what didn't.

question: Is the CRM really free?
reply: Yes. Built on the open-source Twenty CRM — free forever, not a trial. Contacts, companies, pipeline, deals. It manages itself: talk to it, tell it what to do, and it handles follow-ups and updates automatically.

question: How does multi-line dialing work?
reply: Dial up to 3 lines simultaneously. First prospect to answer gets connected, the rest are dropped cleanly. Combined with local presence and spam shield, you get dramatically higher contact rates and call volume.

question: Can I record calls?
reply: Yes — with consent management built in. Recordings feed into AI coaching for post-call analysis and scoring. Review calls, identify patterns, and train your team on what actually works.

question: How does lead automation work?
reply: AI manages your queue — auto-prioritization, re-engagement sequences for leads that didn't answer, and post-sale follow-ups at 1 week, 1 month, and 3 months. Management can disperse thousands of leads across the team automatically.

question: Does Consuelo work with GoHighLevel?
reply: Yes. Consuelo embeds directly inside GHL as a widget — your team never leaves their workflow. Full contact sync, call logging, and pipeline mapping between both platforms.

question: Can I import contacts from Salesforce or HubSpot?
reply: Yes. CSV import with field mapping handles any CRM export. Bring your contacts, companies, and deal data over in minutes. The API also supports programmatic migration for larger datasets.

question: Is there a Zapier integration?
reply: Yes. Connect Consuelo to thousands of apps through Zapier — trigger workflows, sync data, and automate handoffs between your tools.

question: Does Consuelo have an API?
reply: Yes — full REST API plus webhooks. Build custom integrations, sync data with your existing tools, or connect ads platforms straight to your CRM for real-time lead routing.

question: What about team communication tools?
reply: Consuelo connects to Discord, Slack, and Microsoft Teams via Chat SDK. Team management, notifications, and collaboration extend directly into the platforms your team already uses.
